feedback_comment: Output was insufficient to make a call. The interview transcript was incomplete and the AI score felt unreliable. I escalated for a re-interview. Useful to have the flag but the system needs better handling of incomplete session data.

Output was insufficient to make a call. The interview transcript was incomplete and the AI score felt unreliable. I escalated for a re-interview. Useful to have the flag but the system needs better handling of incomplete session data.

Useful output for this candidate. The coding score (72.7%) and the strength summary helped me build the case for the offer quickly. One thing — the system didn't flag that Harsh's notice period is 90 days, which matters for our timeline.

Solid AI output. The logic score of 76.0% was the deciding factor and the system correctly highlighted that. Decision was easy to justify to the panel. The summary was presentable as-is without edits.
Clean output. The confidence score on the rejection (70% range indicators) were helpful. Rejection was straightforward and I didn't need to dig into the raw transcript. AI saved time here.

Agreed with the rejection for Komal. The AI's concern about shallow domain knowledge was accurate — I reviewed the same transcript and the candidate's answers were genuinely surface-level. The output saved me about 40 minutes of manual review.

The hold recommendation was fair but I felt the AI could have been clearer about what specifically needs to be retested. Mihir's hold was vague — I had to design the follow-up interview criteria myself.

Partially useful. The hold recommendation was supported by the scores but the next step was too vague. I expected the system to suggest a specific topic list for the follow-up round given the score breakdown.

Escalated because the AI and I disagreed. Minal's assessment scores didn't fully reflect what I saw in the interview style. Flagged to senior recruiter. The system could benefit from a confidence band indicator to catch these edge cases.
